perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified information is an over-arching term that covers any information, not otherwise indicated above, which the loss of, misuse of, or unauthorized access to or modification of could adversely affect the national interest or the conduct of Federal programs, or the privacy to which individuals are entitled under Section 552a of Title 5, as amended, but which has not been specifically authorized under criteria established by an Executive Order or an Act of Congress to be kept secret in the interest of national defense or foreign policy. This includes information categorized by DHS or other government agencies as: For Official Use Only (FOUO); Official Use Only (OUO); Sensitive Homeland Security Information (SHSI); Limited Official Use (LOU); Law Enforcement Sensitive (LES); Safeguarding Information (SGI); Unclassified Controlled Nuclear Information (UCNI); and any other identifier used by other government agencies to categorize information as sensitive but unclassified.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified information is information which the loss of, misuse of, or unauthorized access to or modification of could adversely affect the national interest or the conduct of Federal programs.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific categories (FOUO, OUO, SHSI, etc.) and reference to Section 552a of Title 5, the definition of SBU is significantly narrowed. This creates an in-text contradiction because the document still uses the term 'SBU' in later sections (e.g., paragraph 5(b)) but without the comprehensive definition originally provided. The definition now lacks specificity, making it harder to determine what information falls under SBU, contradicting the original intent and creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered by this Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of or inadvertent access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information. I agree that I shall return all information to which I have had access or which is in my possession 1) upon demand by an authorized individual; and/or 2) upon the conclusion of my duties, association, or support to DHS; and/or 3) upon the determination that my official duties do not require further access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a comprehensive definition of sensitive but unclassified information (SBU), including specific categories like FOUO, OUO, SHSI, etc., and a reference to Section 552a of Title 5. The changed text significantly narrows this definition by omitting those specific categories and the reference to Section 552a. This omission creates a direct conflict with paragraph 5(b) in the contradicted text, which refers to 'the specific categories of information' when discussing the handling and safeguarding of SBU. Without the original, comprehensive definition, it becomes unclear which categories are included under SBU, making it difficult to comply with the requirements in paragraph 5(b) and creating a strong in-text contradiction as it directly impacts the enforceability and understanding of obligations related to SBU.

perturbation.original_text: I understand that the United States Government may conduct inspections, at any time or place, for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the conditions for access, dissemination, handling and safeguarding information under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand that inspections may be conducted to ensure compliance with the conditions for access, dissemination, handling and safeguarding information under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'the United States Government' may conduct inspections removes the explicit authority and responsibility. This creates uncertainty about who has the power to conduct these inspections. This could contradict the intent of the agreement, as other sections imply Government oversight, particularly concerning enforcement and potential penalties. The contradiction creates uncertainty in paragraph 10, related to who is able to enforce the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the Authorized Entity. By granting me conditional access to information in this context, the United States Government and, with respect to PCII, the Authorized Entity, may seek any remedy available to it to enforce this Agreement including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of information in breach of this Agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, I could be subjected to administrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived any statutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceeding to protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'the United States Government may conduct inspections'. The changed text removes 'the United States Government', creating ambiguity as to who is authorized to conduct inspections. This directly contradicts paragraph 10, which states that the agreement is 'made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government'. By removing the explicit mention of the US Government's inspection authority, the changed text contradicts the enforcement and benefit clauses outlined in paragraph 10, creating uncertainty and a potential in-text contradiction regarding who can enforce the agreement. The Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition clearly applies here, as essential language regarding inspection authority was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.changed_text: I hereby agree that I shall promptly report any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information' and 'I also understand that my anonymity will be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations', creates ambiguity. It removes the clarity on who to report to and the protection of anonymity. This contradicts the expectation of established reporting procedures and potential protection in the original agreement, making Section 10, regarding potential remedies by the government, difficult to enforce without clearly defined reporting steps.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the Authorized Entity. By granting me conditional access to information in this context, the United States Government and, with respect to PCII, the Authorized Entity, may seek any remedy available to it to enforce this Agreement including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of information in breach of this Agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, I could be subjected to administrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived any statutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceeding to protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information' from the Changed Text removes the defined reporting procedure. This contradicts Section 10, which relies on a clear reporting mechanism for the government to enforce remedies in case of a breach of the agreement. Furthermore, the removal of 'I also understand that my anonymity will be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations' introduces ambiguity about the protection of anonymity during reporting, potentially discouraging individuals from reporting violations. This directly impacts the government's ability to enforce the agreement as outlined in Section 10, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information)', the agreement removes the conditions for when the agreement stops being effective. This creates an ambiguity and contradiction with clause 5, since the agreement is to hand over the information upon the conclusion of association with DHS. If it is expected that the agreement be in effect permanently, but also the user is asked to handover the information upon conclusion, it creates an ambiguity in enforcement, because this clause suggests the agreement is for the duration of my acess and forever. Also, it could be said the user is in permanent violation after handing the information over, if there is no end date for the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, whichever occurs first, I will surrender promptly to the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoever that is in my possession. (2) If the Authorized Entity is a United States Government contractor performing services in support of the PCII Program, I will not request, obtain, maintain, or use PCII unless the PCII Program Manager or Program Manager’s designee has first made in writing, with respect to the contractor, the certification as provided for in Section 29.8(c) of the implementing regulations to the CII Act, as amended. (b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered by this Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of or inadvertent access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information. I agree that I shall return all information to which I have had access or which is in my possession 1) upon demand by an authorized individual; and/or 2) upon the conclusion of my duties, association, or support to DHS; and/or 3) upon the determination that my official duties do not require further access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for the agreement to end if released in writing by DHS, but the changed text removes this condition, implying the agreement is perpetual. Clause 5 requires the user to surrender PCII upon completion of engagement with DHS. This is a strong contradiction because the modified clause removes the possibility of ever ending the agreement, while clause 5 suggests that the obligations end. It is a contradiction because the user has to hand over the information, and is still tied by the agreement, creating a situation that is hard to enforce and understand.
